result:
CORS errors mean browser blocked cross-origin request because server did not send right headers.

Common causes:
- server missing Access-Control-Allow-Origin header
- origin mismatch (http vs https, www vs apex, port diff)
- preflight OPTIONS request failing (wrong Allow-Methods or Allow-Headers)
- sending credentials but server returns Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * instead of exact origin
- custom header (Authorization, X-*) not listed in Allow-Headers

Quick diagnosis:
- read exact error text — it names which rule failed
- check Network tab for OPTIONS preflight, look at response headers
- compare request Origin header to server's Allow-Origin response

Fix side:
- CORS must be fixed on server, not client
- if dev only, use proxy (vite/webpack dev proxy) to sidestep
- never "fix" by disabling browser security flags — masks real bug

Paste exact error + request/response headers if want specific answer.
